name: MDInline
comment:
Module MDInline provides one type and two functions. The
type is MDInline, which is the type of inline Markdown elements
such as italic and bold. The `parse` function parses a string
into an MMinline value, a custom type with parts such as
Paragraph, Line, Italic, Bold, Ordered and Unnumbered lists, etc.

The MDInline.parse function is used in the second of the two
parsing operations. A string is first parsed into a hierarchical
list -- a list of strings paired with an integer level.
The hierarchical list is converted to tree. The parser in This
module is mapped over the nodes of the tree to form a new tree.

The MDInline.string function is used to give a string representation
of the BlockMMTree values.

@docs MDInline, parse, string

unions.name: MDInline
unions.comment: The type for inline Markdown elements

values.name: string
values.comment: String representation of an MDInline value

name: Markdown.Elm

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Parse the input and render it to Html, e.g.,

toHtml ExtendedMath "Pythagoras said: $a^2 + b^2 c^2$."

values.name: toHtmlWithExternaTOC
values.comment:
Like `toHtmlWithTOC`, but constructs returns a record,
one field of which is the rendered document,
anther of which is the rendered table of contents.
values.type: Markdown.Option.Option -> String.String -> { title : Html.Html msg, toc : Html.Html msg, document : Html.Html msg }

values.name: toHtmlWithTOC
values.comment: Like `toHtml`, but constructs a table of contents.

name: Markdown.Option

unions.name: Option
unions.comment:
**Description:**

  - Standard: vanilla Markdown
  - Extended: strike-through text, poetry and verbatim blocks
  - ExtendMath: like Extended, but TeX formulas are rendered

name: Parse
comment:
The purpose of this module is to parse a Document,
that is, a string, into an abstract syntax tree (AST)
which can then be further transformed or passed on
to a rendering function.  The AST is a rose tree
of `MBlock` -- short for "Markdown Blocks."


@docs toMDBlockTree,  MDBlock, BlockContent, stringOfMDBlockTree

unions.name: BlockContent
unions.comment: The type of a parsed Block

unions.name: MDBlock
unions.comment:
An MBlock differs from the a Block
in that the Content, which is a
type alias for String,
has been parsed into a BlockContent value
by applying

    MDInline.parse : Option -> String -> MDInline

values.name: stringOfMDBlockTree
values.comment:
A string representation of an MDBlockTree.  Useful
for verifying the validity of the AST.

values.name: toMDBlockTree
values.comment:
Parse a string using a Markdown flavor option, returning the AST.
Example:

    parseToMDBlockTree Extended "This **is** a test."
    --> Tree (MDBlock (MarkdownBlock Root) 0 (M (Paragraph [Line [OrdinaryText "DOCUMENT"]]))) [Tree (MDBlock (MarkdownBlock Plain) 1 (M (Paragraph [Line [OrdinaryText ("This "),BoldText ("is "),OrdinaryText ("a test.")],Line []]))) []]
